_schema.description: Multi-track timeline of key events in humanitarian response history 1991–2024. Four thematic tracks with both point events (single date) and span events (start–end duration). Fictional structuring of plausible historical events — proves the timeline renders before real data is substituted.

fields.domain: array [ISO date, ISO date] — x-axis start and end
fields.today: string ISO date — draws a reference line at this date
fields.tracks[].label: string — track name displayed in left header column
fields.tracks[].color: string — hex color from/derived from hai palette
fields.tracks[].events[].label: string — short label displayed on chart (≤18 chars)
fields.tracks[].events[].description: string — full detail shown in tooltip
fields.tracks[].events[].date: string ISO date — used for type='point' events
fields.tracks[].events[].start: string ISO date — start of type='span' events
fields.tracks[].events[].end: string ISO date — end of type='span' events

_schema.replace: Replace tracks[] and their events[] with your own data. Mix 'point' and 'span' types freely. The chart derives its xScale domain from the domain[] field — update it to match your time range.

events.label: UN Res. 46/182
events.description: UN General Assembly Resolution 46/182 establishes OCHA and the modern humanitarian coordination framework.

events.label: IDRL Drafting
events.description: International Disaster Response Law negotiations and drafting of core principles for cross-border humanitarian access.

events.label: Cluster System
events.description: IASC humanitarian reform launches the cluster approach — coordinating actors by sector (shelter, health, food, WASH, etc.).

events.label: World Hum. Summit
events.description: First World Humanitarian Summit in Istanbul. Commitments include the Grand Bargain and New Way of Working.

events.label: Grand Bargain Review
events.description: Mid-term review of 2016 Grand Bargain commitments on localization, transparency, and efficiency.

events.label: AI Governance
events.description: UN Secretary-General's AI Advisory Body issues recommendations; humanitarian agencies adopt first AI governance frameworks.

events.label: ReliefWeb Launch
events.description: OCHA launches ReliefWeb — first major online humanitarian information platform, aggregating situation reports and maps.

events.label: HDX Launch
events.description: Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX) goes live, providing open humanitarian datasets and interoperability standards.

events.label: Open Data Initiative
events.description: GFDRR and World Bank drive open data for disaster resilience; first structured humanitarian data standards adopted.

events.label: AI Pilots
events.description: First systematic AI applications in refugee registration (UNHCR), needs assessment (WFP), and predictive analytics (OCHA).

events.label: COVID Digital Response
events.description: Rapid digitisation of humanitarian coordination during COVID-19: remote sensing, digital cash, contactless registration.

events.label: HAI Frameworks
events.description: Major humanitarian agencies adopt shared frameworks for responsible AI in humanitarian response and refugee contexts.

events.label: Rwanda Crisis
events.description: Genocide and refugee crisis; 2 million people displaced in 100 days — largest and fastest refugee movement ever recorded.

events.label: Indian Ocean Tsunami
events.description: Magnitude 9.1 earthquake triggers tsunami across 14 countries. 230,000 deaths; $7 billion humanitarian response mobilised.

events.label: Haiti EQ Response
events.description: Haiti earthquake: largest urban humanitarian operation in history; 10 million affected, 1.5 million initially displaced.

events.label: Typhoon Haiyan
events.description: Super Typhoon Haiyan in the Philippines — 6,300 deaths, 14 million affected. Became benchmark for rapid onset response.

events.label: DRC Ebola
events.description: Second-largest Ebola outbreak in history, in a conflict-affected zone. 3,400+ deaths; first major AI-assisted health response.

events.label: Ukraine Crisis
events.description: Russia's full-scale invasion triggers fastest European displacement since WWII: 8 million external refugees within 3 months.

events.label: Turkey-Syria EQ
events.description: 7.8 magnitude earthquake strikes Turkey and Syria. 50,000+ deaths; 9 million displaced. Record response in first 72 hours.

events.label: GHD Principles
events.description: 23 donor governments adopt the Good Humanitarian Donorship (GHD) principles — first global framework for principled funding.

events.label: CERF Established
events.description: UN Central Emergency Response Fund (CERF) formally established with $450M target — pooled funding for rapid onset crises.

events.label: CERF Scale-up
events.description: CERF expands dramatically post-Haiti; record disbursements. CBPF country-level pooled funds also established.

events.label: Grand Bargain
events.description: Grand Bargain signed by 51 donors and aid organisations — commitments on transparency, cash, and localisation.

events.label: COVID Funding Surge
events.description: Global humanitarian funding reaches record $28.9B in 2020; COVID triggers emergency reprogramming across all portfolios.

events.label: Financing Reform
events.description: Major OCHA-led review of humanitarian financing architecture; proposals for sovereign debt relief and climate-humanitarian links.

events.label: AI Funding Tools
events.description: First AI-driven resource allocation models piloted by CERF and major CBPFs; predictive needs-based distribution tested.
